raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "SB 213 will modernize outdated California law by making it easier for registered nurses and other health care workers to access the workers’ compensation system.",
    "SB 213 addresses a glaring inequity in California law regarding workers' compensation for healthcare workers compared to male-dominated professions such as police officers and firefighters.",
    "Nurses often face significant barriers to receiving paid time off for work-related illnesses or injuries.",
    "In 2020, workplace hazards that required at least one day off work totaled 78,740 for registered nurses, compared to 220 for firefighters and 200 for police officers.",
    "SB 213 will ensure that nurses and other healthcare workers receive streamlined eligibility for workers’ compensation for issues such as COVID-19, respiratory diseases, cancer, PTSD, and musculoskeletal injuries."
  ]
}
```
